description: Looking at the unwired queue surfaced two more Trunks-depicting corpus cards that my E2 caveat had explicitly called out as 'not in corpus' — wrongly. TB2-007 Tiny Rivals Trunks depicts Kid Trunks (the fourth named incarnation: present-timeline child Trunks raised on Earth, designer-coordinated paired-print with TB2-004 Tiny Rivals Son Goten as the canonical Junior Tournament Buu-Saga cohort). BT2-063 Father-Son Galick Gun depicts adult Future Trunks alongside Vegeta executing the DBS episode 65 canonical father-son attack (SS Future Trunks + SS Blue Vegeta duo portrait). Both wire cleanly to the parent Trunks node under existing structural precedent (Future Trunks aspect for BT2-063; new Kid Trunks aspect for TB2-007). E3 extends the parent's appears_on from 7 to 9 cards and clarifies that all four canonical Trunks incarnations are now corpus-represented.
structural_precedent_cited: tamiyo (the same node accumulates aspects as corpus members arrive)
canonical_sources: Dragon Ball Wiki — Father-Son Galick Gun
Bandai dbs-cardgame.com TB2 promo set listings
files_modified: cards/_characters/trunks.md (extended appears_on from 7 to 9 cards; updated intro to four-incarnation framing; new Aspects sub-sections for Kid Trunks and Future-Trunks-alongside-Vegeta; updated Discrete Lair count and Caveats)
voice_note: I had to fix my own Mr. Nodeley E2 caveat that wrongly claimed Kid Trunks wasn't in corpus. Walking the unwired queue surfaced TB2-007 (Kid Trunks) and BT2-063 (Future Trunks with Vegeta in canonical Father-Son Galick Gun pose) — both already vision-verified, both unambiguously Trunks, both waiting to be wired. The Discrete Lair stack thickens from 7 corpus anchors to 9 across four named incarnations. The corrective is small but load-bearing — the Trunks node's claim 'all four canonical incarnations are corpus-represented' is now true. This is exactly why Edgelord values the audit-then-extension pattern.
